task_completion.description: Access or read the trial-level recognition memory CSV data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the agent opening 'recognition_memory_data.csv' in a text editor, spreadsheet application, or loading it via a script (e.g., pandas read_csv).

task_completion.description: Identify and exclude the contaminated participant based on impossible RTs or perfect accuracy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing code or data filtering steps that explicitly remove 'sub-99999', filter out RTs between 25-45ms, or filter out 100% accuracy rows.

task_completion.description: Calculate d-prime (sensitivity) and criterion (response bias) for the valid participants.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the mathematical formulas or code (e.g., using scipy.stats.norm.ppf in Python or equivalent in R/spreadsheets) computing Hit Rates, False Alarm Rates, d-prime, and criterion.

task_completion.description: Format the calculated SDT metrics as a JSON summary report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the construction of a JSON object/dictionary containing participant IDs mapped to their respective d-prime and criterion values.

task_completion.description: Save the final JSON report to the specified analysis directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the file being saved exactly at '~/pebl/analysis/sdt_report.json' or '/home/ga/pebl/analysis/sdt_report.json' (e.g., via a save dialog, terminal command, or script output).

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually calculated the metrics rather than hallucinating or hardcoding the final JSON.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a script (Python, R) or spreadsheet containing actual SDT formulas (z-transforms of hit/false alarm rates) being executed.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the provided local dataset.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the file path '~/pebl/data/recognition_memory_data.csv' is used as the data source, rather than downloading external data.

integrity.description: Verify the JSON report was actually saved to the file system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal commands (like 'ls ~/pebl/analysis'), file explorer views, or script completion messages confirming the file 'sdt_report.json' exists on disk, not just printed to standard output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains exactly 14 valid participants (sub-10159 through sub-10172) and 1 contaminated participant (sub-99999). The contaminated participant has physiologically impossible 25-45ms response times and perfect accuracy, and MUST be excluded from the final report. The final output must be a JSON file located exactly at /home/ga/pebl/analysis/sdt_report.json containing d-prime and criterion values only for the 14 valid participants.
